
2. Materials and Methods

2.1 Study Site and Soil Sampling

Soil and rhizosphere samples were collected from a wheat field in northern Israel during the
vegetative growth stage. To obtain rhizosphere soil, plants were carefully uprooted and the
loosely adhering soil was removed by gentle shaking. The tightly adhering rhizosphere soil
was then collected by shaking the roots vigorously and brushing off the remaining soil. Bulk
soil was collected from inter-row positions at least 30 cm from any plant. Samples were
collected from 0-10 cm depth using a sterile spatula, immediately placed in sterile 50 mL
Falcon tubes, and transported on dry ice to the laboratory within 4 hours.

2.2 DNA Extraction and Sequencing

DNA was extracted from 0.25 g of each rhizosphere soil sample using the DNeasy PowerSoil
Pro Kit (Qiagen) following the manufacturer's instructions. DNA concentration and quality
were assessed using a NanoDrop spectrophotometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Samples
with A260/A280 ratios between 1.7 and 2.0 were used for downstream analyses. The 16S
rRNA gene (V3-V4 region) was amplified using primers 341F and 806R in triplicate reactions.

2.3 Bioinformatics Analysis

Paired-end reads were quality-trimmed with Trimmomatic v0.39. Amplicon sequence variants
(ASVs) were inferred with DADA2. Taxonomy was assigned against the SILVA 138 database.

3. Results
